data.title: code-reviewer-light (haiku): structurally-valid bash test code -> false-positive correctness/maintainability findings (reproduced 2x in single session)
data.description:
## Incident Overview
Sprint 2026-04-19 (epics ca48-c3c3 + db68-ca95): code-reviewer-light (haiku model) emitted high-confidence false-positive findings on bash structural boundary tests on 2 separate dispatches.

## Reproduced Cases
1. tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-pattern-reference.sh — reviewer claimed `[[ "$_has_familiarity" == "1" && ( "$_has_low" == "1" || "$_has_medium" == "1" ) ]]` had a precedence bug because parens were "command substitution". Bash `[[ ]]` DOES support parentheses for logical grouping (verified by direct execution: matched=true). Re-dispatched to opus → score 5/0 findings.
2. tests/skills/test-preplanning-research-findings-schema.sh — reviewer claimed test file was not staged or present. `git status` confirmed file IS staged and present in worktree. Reviewer was likely confused by orchestrator CWD vs worktree CWD. Re-dispatched to opus → score 5/0 findings.

## Pattern
Both findings were "important" severity with confident wording. Both were demonstrably false on direct verification. Pattern: haiku reviewer makes confident incorrect claims about bash language semantics or filesystem state, requiring opus re-dispatch to clear.

## Cost
- 2 wasted reviewer dispatches in 1 session
- Manual verification time
- Risk that orchestrators acting on findings without verification will introduce false fixes (e.g., the "fix" suggested for bash parens would have made the test syntactically equivalent but worse-styled)

## Expected Behavior
Light reviewer should defer or escalate when uncertain about language semantics or filesystem state, not emit confident findings.

## Actual Behavior
Light reviewer emits confident findings with severity=important even when the underlying claim is demonstrably false.

## Suggested Fix
- Add a guardrail in the haiku reviewer prompt: for bash `[[ ]]` syntax, do not flag parentheses as command substitution
- Add a guardrail: do not claim a file is "not present" without explicit confirmation; defer to a NEEDS_HUMAN classification when filesystem state is ambiguous
- Track false-positive rate via review-stats.sh and consider model upgrade if the rate exceeds N% per session

## Workaround
Re-dispatch to opus on demonstrable false positives. Already adopted as standing practice this session via memory feedback_opus_for_sonnet_subagents.md (with carve-out: light stays haiku unless re-review needed).
